data.title: The three-year shareholder returns and company earnings persist lower as H&K (EPA:MLHK) stock falls a further 11% in past week
data.description:
The truth is that if you invest for long enough, you're going to end up with some losing stocks. Long term H&K AG (EPA:MLHK) shareholders know that all too well, since the share price is down considerably over three years. Sadly for them, the share price is down 68% in that time. The more recent news is of little comfort, with the share price down 66% in a year. Shareholders have had an even rougher run lately, with the share price down 17% in the last 90 days.

With the stock having lost 11% in the past week, it's worth taking a look at business performance and seeing if there's any red flags.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

In his essay The Superinvestors of Graham-and-Doddsville Warren Buffett described how share prices do not always rationally reflect the value of a business. One imperfect but simple way to consider how the market perception of a company has shifted is to compare the change in the earnings per share (EPS) with the share price movement.

H&K saw its EPS decline at a compound rate of 1.7% per year, over the last three years. This reduction in EPS is slower than the 31% annual reduction in the share price. So it seems the market was too confident about the business, in the past.

The image below shows how EPS has tracked over time (if you click on the image you can see greater detail).

Before buying or selling a stock, we always recommend a close examination of historic growth trends, available here.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

It is important to consider the total shareholder return, as well as the share price return, for any given stock. The TSR is a return calculation that accounts for the value of cash dividends (assuming that any dividend received was reinvested) and the calculated value of any discounted capital raisings and spin-offs. Arguably, the TSR gives a more comprehensive picture of the return generated by a stock. As it happens, H&K's TSR for the last 3 years was -60%, which exceeds the share price return mentioned earlier. This is largely a result of its dividend payments!

A Different Perspective

Investors in H&K had a tough year, with a total loss of 66% (including dividends), against a market gain of about 3.9%. Even the share prices of good stocks drop sometimes, but we want to see improvements in the fundamental metrics of a business, before getting too interested. Unfortunately, last year's performance may indicate unresolved challenges, given that it was worse than the annualised loss of 8% over the last half decade. Generally speaking long term share price weakness can be a bad sign, though contrarian investors might want to research the stock in hope of a turnaround. data.title: Is There An Opportunity With China Gas Holdings Limited's (HKG:384) 49% Undervaluation?
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • The projected fair value for China Gas Holdings is HK$15.37 based on 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity
  • China Gas Holdings is estimated to be 49% undervalued based on current share price of HK$7.80
  • Our fair value estimate is 89% higher than China Gas Holdings' analyst price target of HK$8.12

How far off is China Gas Holdings Limited (HKG:384) from its intrinsic value? Using the most recent financial data, we'll take a look at whether the stock is fairly priced by estimating the company's future cash flows and discounting them to their present value. We will take advantage of the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model for this purpose. Don't get put off by the jargon, the math behind it is actually quite straightforward.

Remember though, that there are many ways to estimate a company's value, and a DCF is just one method. For those who are keen learners of equity analysis, the Simply Wall St analysis model here may be something of interest to you.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

What's The Estimated Valuation?

We are going to use a two-stage DCF model, which, as the name states, takes into account two stages of growth. The first stage is generally a higher growth period which levels off heading towards the terminal value, captured in the second 'steady growth' period. In the first stage we need to estimate the cash flows to the business over the next ten years. Where possible we use analyst estimates, but when these aren't available we extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the last estimate or reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

A DCF is all about the idea that a dollar in the future is less valuable than a dollar today, so we need to discount the sum of these future cash flows to arrive at a present value estimate:

10-year free cash flow (FCF) forecast

2026202720282029203020312032203320342035
Levered FCF (HK$, Millions) HK$3.22bHK$4.94bHK$4.89bHK$4.90bHK$4.95bHK$5.03bHK$5.12bHK$5.24bHK$5.36bHK$5.50b
Growth Rate Estimate SourceAnalyst x3Analyst x4Analyst x3Est @ 0.23%Est @ 1.00%Est @ 1.55%Est @ 1.93%Est @ 2.20%Est @ 2.38%Est @ 2.51%
Present Value (HK$, Millions) Discounted @ 7.9% HK$3.0kHK$4.2kHK$3.9kHK$3.6kHK$3.4kHK$3.2kHK$3.0kHK$2.8kHK$2.7kHK$2.6k

("Est" = FCF growth rate estimated by Simply Wall St)
Present Value of 10-year Cash Flow (PVCF) = HK$32b

After calculating the present value of future cash flows in the initial 10-year period, we need to calculate the Terminal Value, which accounts for all future cash flows beyond the first stage. For a number of reasons a very conservative growth rate is used that cannot exceed that of a country's GDP growth. In this case we have used the 5-year average of the 10-year government bond yield (2.8%) to estimate future growth. In the same way as with the 10-year 'growth' period, we discount future cash flows to today's value, using a cost of equity of 7.9%.

Terminal Value (TV)= FCF2035 × (1 + g) ÷ (r – g) = HK$5.5b× (1 + 2.8%) ÷ (7.9%– 2.8%) = HK$110b

Present Value of Terminal Value (PVTV)= TV / (1 + r)10= HK$110b÷ ( 1 + 7.9%)10= HK$51b

The total value, or equity value, is then the sum of the present value of the future cash flows, which in this case is HK$84b. To get the intrinsic value per share, we divide this by the total number of shares outstanding. Relative to the current share price of HK$7.8, the company appears quite undervalued at a 49% discount to where the stock price trades currently. The assumptions in any calculation have a big impact on the valuation, so it is better to view this as a rough estimate, not precise down to the last cent.

Important Assumptions

Now the most important inputs to a discounted cash flow are the discount rate, and of course, the actual cash flows. You don't have to agree with these inputs, I recommend redoing the calculations yourself and playing with them. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at China Gas Holdings as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. In this calculation we've used 7.9%, which is based on a levered beta of 1.004. Beta is a measure of a stock's volatility, compared to the market as a whole. We get our beta from the industry average beta of globally comparable companies, with an imposed limit between 0.8 and 2.0, which is a reasonable range for a stable business.

See our latest analysis for China Gas Holdings

SWOT Analysis for China Gas Holdings

Strength
  • Debt is well covered by earnings.
    Balance sheet summary for 384.
Weakness
  • Earnings declined over the past year.
  • Dividend is low compared to the top 25% of dividend payers in the Gas Utilities market.
Opportunity
  • Annual earnings are forecast to grow for the next 3 years.
  • Trading below our estimate of fair value by more than 20%.
Threat
  • Debt is not well covered by operating cash flow.
  • Dividends are not covered by earnings and cashflows.
  • Annual earnings are forecast to grow slower than the Hong Kong market.
    Is 384 well equipped to handle threats?

Moving On:

Whilst important, the DCF calculation ideally won't be the sole piece of analysis you scrutinize for a company. DCF models are not the be-all and end-all of investment valuation. Preferably you'd apply different cases and assumptions and see how they would impact the company's valuation. For instance, if the terminal value growth rate is adjusted slightly, it can dramatically alter the overall result. What is the reason for the share price sitting below the intrinsic value? For China Gas Holdings, we've put together three further items you should assess:

  1. Risks: Consider for instance, the ever-present spectre of investment risk. We've identified 2 warning signs with China Gas Holdings , and understanding them should be part of your investment process.
  2. Future Earnings: How does 384's growth rate compare to its peers and the wider market? Dig deeper into the analyst consensus number for the upcoming years by interacting with our free analyst growth expectation chart.
  3. Other Solid Businesses: Low debt, high returns on equity and good past performance are fundamental to a strong business. Why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals to see if there are other companies you may not have considered!

• Amazon.com (NasdaqGS:AMZN) is launching its largest-ever round of corporate layoffs, targeting up to 30,000 roles.
  • The reductions span AWS, Prime Video, and human resources, alongside a broader restructuring of teams and budgets.
  • The company is reallocating resources toward automation and artificial intelligence projects across its operations.

For you as an investor, this move sits at the intersection of Amazon's core lines of business, including e commerce, cloud computing, and digital media. The decision to reshape headcount across AWS and Prime Video, as well as support functions, highlights how central automation and AI have become to the way NasdaqGS:AMZN runs its platforms and services.

Layoffs on this scale can change how the company allocates capital, manages costs, and prioritizes new products. As the restructuring plays out, you may want to watch how Amazon explains its automation and AI spending, and how those choices affect its position in retail, cloud, and content over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Amazon.com by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Amazon.com.

How Amazon.com stacks up against its biggest competitors

The new wave of layoffs and restructuring signals that Amazon’s leadership is reshaping how decisions get made, particularly in AWS and Prime Video, as more functions are handed to automation and AI systems. For you, this points to a leadership team that is willing to trade organizational size and redundant management layers for leaner structures that can support large AI capital projects, including AWS initiatives like the European Sovereign Cloud.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Amazon.com Narrative

Recent commentary around Amazon often revolves around AWS as the profit engine, rising AI related capital spending, and the push to apply AI across retail, advertising, and logistics. This restructuring is likely to feed that narrative by showing management is willing to reallocate human and financial resources away from slower return areas and toward AI infrastructure and services that sit at the center of many bullish and cautious views on the stock.

Amazon.com, Weighing The Risks And Rewards

  • ⚠️ Higher AI and data center capital expenditure, including deals like the Rio Tinto copper supply and sovereign cloud build out, could pressure free cash flow if returns arrive slowly.
  • ⚠️ Legal and regulatory overhangs, such as the FTC antitrust case and labor related lawsuits, add uncertainty around Amazon’s future structure and cost base.
  • 🎁 Management’s focus on AWS, AI infrastructure, and advertising suggests continued prioritization of business lines that have been important to profitability and margins.
  • 🎁 Streamlining corporate roles and layers may support efficiency gains as Amazon rolls out automation and AI across retail, media, and cloud operations.

What To Watch Next

Over the next few quarters, you may want to track how leadership explains the balance between rising AI investment, cost savings from layoffs, and any legal or regulatory developments, especially around the October 2026 FTC trial. You can also see how other investors are interpreting these moves by reading community views in this narrative hub.

data.title: FFB Bancorp (OTCPK:FFBB) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Valuation Narrative After FY 2025 Results
data.description:
FFB Bancorp (FFBB) just wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of US$19.4 million and basic EPS of US$1.08, setting the tone for how the full year is being assessed. Over recent quarters, revenue has moved from US$26.6 million in Q4 2024 to US$27.3 million in Q1 2025, US$24.2 million in Q2 2025 and US$22.8 million in Q3 2025, while quarterly EPS shifted from US$3.06 to US$2.56, US$1.95 and US$2.07 across the same stretch. Taken together with the latest trailing 12 month totals, these results put the spotlight on how FFB Bancorp is managing profitability and margins through the year.

See our full analysis for FFB Bancorp.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings print lines up against the main narratives around FFB Bancorp, highlighting where the story is supported by the data and where it starts to look less convincing.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Net margin at 25.2% on trailing basis

  • Over the last 12 months, FFB Bancorp posted a net profit margin of 25.2%, compared with 34.5% a year earlier, while trailing net income was US$23.6 million on US$93.7 million of revenue.
  • What stands out for a bullish argument around earnings quality is that 5 year earnings growth averaged 14.3% per year, yet the latest trailing margin is lower than the prior year, which means:
    • Supporters pointing to that 14.3% annual growth can still lean on a multi year record, but the 25.2% vs 34.5% margin gap shows recent profitability has been less strong than that history.
    • Anyone cautious about the bullish view can point to the FY 2025 quarterly pattern, where net income excluding extra items stepped down from US$9.7 million in Q4 2024 to US$3.2 million in Q4 2025 alongside lower revenue.
To see how this earnings profile lines up with a full narrative, check what the market is focusing on behind these numbers. 📊 Read the full FFB Bancorp Consensus Narrative.

Loan book growth with higher problem loans

  • Total loans in the quarterly data moved from US$998.2 million in Q3 2024 to US$1.12b in Q3 2025, while reported non performing loans in that same quarter were US$26.9 million, or 2.4% of loans, with only 57% allowance coverage flagged as low by the thresholds used.
  • Bears focus on credit quality, and the figures here give them specific talking points:
    • Critics highlight that non performing loans at 2.4% are tagged as high, compared with lower dollar amounts earlier in the timeline, which can draw attention when set against the growth in the loan book above US$1.1b.
    • They also point to the 57% allowance coverage of those bad loans, described as low, which means a meaningful portion of the US$26.9 million in non performing loans is not backed by reserves in these metrics.
Skeptical investors are watching those credit numbers closely, especially as the loan book has grown. 🐻 FFB Bancorp Bear Case

P/E of 10.8x with large DCF gap

  • FFB Bancorp trades on a trailing P/E of 10.8x, compared with 13.1x for peers and 11.8x for the wider US Banks group, and the current share price of US$85.10 sits about 63.6% below a stated DCF fair value of US$233.86.
  • Supporters of a bullish angle on valuation lean heavily on this discount, but the operating data adds useful context:
    • On one hand, the lower P/E versus both peers and the industry, combined with the gap to the DCF fair value, lines up with the idea that the stock screens as inexpensive relative to recent earnings and to that intrinsic value estimate.
    • On the other hand, the same trailing set of numbers includes a reduced net margin of 25.2% versus 34.5% a year earlier, which gives more cautious investors a reason to question how much weight to put on that valuation gap without seeing how profitability evolves.

data.title: Novo Nordisk Expands Obesity Pill Reach While Targeting Curative Diabetes Therapies
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk (CPSE:NOVO B) received FDA approval for an oral Wegovy pill for weight management and has launched it in the US with major retail partners.
  • The company is reporting rapid early prescription uptake for the Wegovy pill soon after launch.
  • Novo Nordisk expanded its cell therapy collaboration with Aspect Biosystems to pursue potentially curative treatments for diabetes.

Novo Nordisk, best known for its diabetes and obesity treatments, is pushing further into weight management with the first oral version of a proven obesity therapy. The Wegovy pill arrives at a time when demand for weight loss drugs is already drawing strong interest from patients, healthcare providers and retailers. For investors, it adds another format to the company’s obesity franchise while keeping the focus on prescription medicines rather than consumer supplements.

At the same time, the broadened cell therapy work with Aspect Biosystems points to a longer term effort in diabetes care that goes beyond glucose control. The company is committing capital and research attention to therapies that aim at disease modification, not just symptom management, which may reshape its risk profile and research pipeline mix over time.

How Novo Nordisk stacks up against its biggest competitors

The Wegovy pill launch gives Novo Nordisk another way to reach the weight management market, and partnering with retailers like Amazon and Costco Wholesale brings the product closer to everyday consumer shopping habits. Early prescription volume in the thousands within weeks suggests strong initial interest, which may support the company’s existing GLP 1 franchise and deepen relationships with prescribers and payers who are already familiar with its injectable therapies.

Advertisement

Novo Nordisk Narrative, from GLP 1 leader to broader metabolic player

The expanded Aspect Biosystems deal feeds into a longer running narrative of Novo Nordisk trying to move from chronic symptom control in diabetes to potentially curative approaches. By taking equity, technology and royalty exposure rather than full in house build out, the company is adding a high risk, long dated optionality layer on top of its established obesity and diabetes drug base, which some investors see as a way to diversify future treatment types while keeping near term focus on marketed products like Wegovy.

Risks and rewards around these launches

  • Wegovy pill and retailer partnerships broaden access points for patients, which could support prescription volumes if supply and reimbursement hold up.
  • The Aspect collaboration structure gives Novo Nordisk upside through milestones and royalties on potential future products without carrying all development and manufacturing obligations.
  • Legal challenges, including antitrust and other lawsuits related to GLP 1s, introduce headline and financial risk that could interact with how aggressively the company defends its market share.
  • Cell therapy for diabetes is technically complex and long term, so timelines, costs and eventual regulatory outcomes remain uncertain even with the expanded partnership.

What to watch next

From here, you might focus on how quickly Wegovy pill prescriptions build over the next few quarters, how retailers manage inventory and access, and whether there are early clinical or regulatory milestones from the Aspect partnership that signal progress on cell based diabetes treatments. Why Erste Group Bank’s new Tier 2 issue matters for equity investors

Erste Group Bank (WBAG:EBS) has just completed a €750 million fixed to floating rate subordinated Tier 2 notes issue, a move that directly affects its capital structure and, by extension, how you might think about the equity.

The notes are callable, subordinated and carry a variable coupon, sitting below senior debt but above common equity in the capital stack. That position can influence loss absorption, regulatory capital treatment and the bank’s flexibility around future lending capacity.

Because this is a Tier 2 instrument, it counts toward the bank’s regulatory capital, which is one of the key reference points for supervisors and rating agencies. For equity holders, the question is how this extra layer of subordinated funding fits alongside profitability, returns and the recent share price move.

  • The size of the issue at €750 million and 100% price sets a clear funding benchmark for this part of Erste’s capital.
  • The fixed to floating structure means the bank’s interest expense on this instrument will adjust over time and can affect net interest income sensitivity to rate changes.
  • The 2036 maturity and call features influence refinancing risk and the timing of any future capital management decisions.

Before you judge what this means for the stock, it helps to put the new issue next to Erste’s current income profile. The bank reports revenue of €10,964.0 million and net income of €3,046.91 million, figures that frame how manageable the additional coupon burden might be within its broader earnings base.

Equity investors also often look at how capital actions line up with share performance. Over the past month, Erste Group Bank shares have returned 6.92%, while the past 3 months show a 28.46% return and the past year an 81.21% total return. Those moves sit alongside a reported 14.11% revenue growth rate and 11.50% net income growth rate on an annual basis.
